# Per-host process-execution burst detection with severity tiers (T1059)

**Dataset**: `xdr_data`

Detects abnormal process-execution bursts per host by time-bucketing PROCESS_START events into 5-minute windows and counting executions per host per bucket. A nested `if()` assigns severity tiers from the per-bucket exec count, and a `concat`-built `alert_key` (host@time=sev) deduplicates one alert per host/bucket. Note `bin` is a standalone stage that mutates `_time` in place before the `comp ... by ... _time`, and `dedup ... by desc execs` keeps the highest-count row per alert key.

```sql
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START and agent_hostname != null and actor_process_image_name != null
| alter bin_lc = lowercase(actor_process_image_name)
| bin _time span = 5m
| comp count() as execs, count_distinct(bin_lc) as distinct_bins by agent_hostname, _time
| alter sev = if(execs > 100, "critical", if(execs > 50, "high", if(execs > 20, "medium", "low")))
| alter alert_key = concat(agent_hostname, "@", to_string(_time), "=", sev)
| filter sev != "low"
| dedup alert_key by desc execs
| sort desc execs
| fields agent_hostname, _time, execs, distinct_bins, sev, alert_key
| limit 50
```
